name: public_ir_subset
description: A small public-style IR benchmark subset (MTEB/BEIR-style query/relevant-doc pairs). Generic, domain-neutral retrieval items used to measure a candidate embedding model's BASELINE retrieval capability. Contains no private data of any kind: all text is generic encyclopedic/technical prose. The public-vs-Engram delta against this baseline is the calibration signal.

docs.text: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose, releasing oxygen as a byproduct.
Chlorophyll is the pigment in plant cells that absorbs light, primarily in the blue and red wavelengths, and reflects green light.
The water cycle describes the continuous movement of water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection across the planet.
A relational database stores data in tables of rows and columns and supports queries written in a structured query language.
An index in a database is a data structure that speeds up row lookups at the cost of additional storage and slower writes.
A hash table maps keys to values using a hash function and offers average constant-time insertion and lookup.
The mitochondrion is an organelle that generates most of a cell's supply of chemical energy through respiration.
Tectonic plates are large slabs of the planet's outer shell that move slowly and reshape continents and oceans over geological time.
An earthquake occurs when stored stress along a fault is released suddenly, sending seismic waves through the surrounding rock.
A compiler translates source code written in a high-level programming language into machine code that a processor can execute.
An interpreter executes program instructions directly, line by line, without first producing a separate machine-code file.
The greenhouse effect warms a planet when atmospheric gases trap outgoing infrared radiation near the surface.
Vaccines train the immune system to recognize a pathogen by exposing it to a harmless fragment or weakened form of that pathogen.
A neural network learns by adjusting connection weights to reduce the error between its predictions and labeled training examples.
Gradient descent is an optimization method that iteratively moves parameters in the direction that most reduces a loss function.
The speed of light in a vacuum is a fundamental constant and the maximum speed at which information can travel.
Supply and demand describes how the price of a good settles where the quantity buyers want equals the quantity sellers offer.
A glacier is a persistent body of dense ice that forms where snow accumulation exceeds melting over many years.
DNA stores genetic information as a sequence of four nucleotide bases arranged along a double helix.
A bridge distributes load to its supports through tension and compression in its structural members.

queries.text: How do plants turn sunlight into energy?
How does water move around the planet?
How is structured data stored and queried?
How can lookups in a table be made faster?
Where does a cell get most of its energy?
What is the difference between compiling and interpreting code?
Why is the planet getting warmer?
How do vaccines protect against disease?
How does a model learn from examples?
How are prices set in a free market?
How does a structure carry weight to the ground?
